hypotheses.label: Literal
hypotheses.deconstruct: Message contains two statements: (1) a process timed out, (2) an Excel file should be attached to the Slack channel output. The Slack snippet shows a forecast bot responded to @JCE's status request with 'Working on it...' — implying forecast output was posted but the Excel was not included. Key entities: Excel file (artifact), Slack channel (destination), forecast bot output (existing message). No file path specified. No channel name specified. Output requirement: Excel appears as attachment in Slack alongside or following the forecast status message.
hypotheses.diagnose: Two gaps block clean execution: (a) the Excel file path is unspecified — must be located before attaching, (b) the target Slack channel name/ID is unspecified — must be resolved from the bot context. The timeout mentioned is context, not a question — no explanation is expected. The word 'also' implies the forecast text already posted; the ask is additive: add the Excel on top.
hypotheses.develop: Technical request type → constraint-based + precision focus. Role: executor with Slack MCP access. Steps: (1) locate the most recent forecast Excel file on EC2 or in Google Drive, (2) resolve the Slack channel where the forecast bot posted, (3) upload/attach the Excel to that channel. No commentary on the timeout unless asked.
hypotheses.deliver: Locate the most recently generated forecast Excel file (check /tmp, ~/forecast outputs, or Google Drive). Identify the Slack channel where the forecast bot posted the @JCE status message. Use slack_send_message or equivalent to post the Excel as an attachment to that channel. Confirm the file landed with a one-line status. Do not explain the timeout.

hypotheses.label: Identity-shaped
hypotheses.deconstruct: Justs is in early days at Jeff, time-poor, zero patience for incomplete work or cognitive overhead (FUS046, FUS047). The forecast bot is a tool he relies on for work deliverables. 'Attach the excel also' is a standing correction to the output format — he expected the Excel to accompany the forecast report automatically, and it didn't. The 'timed out' prefix is acknowledgment of a prior failure, not a question. He wants the current gap closed AND the behavior corrected going forward.
hypotheses.diagnose: The identity pattern is: results-only, no options surfaced, no deliberation. The gap is that the forecast Slack output does not include the Excel as standard. Justs is not asking for a one-time manual attach — he's implicitly asking for the delivery format to be fixed so this never comes up again. Missing: whether this is a one-off fix or a workflow change.
hypotheses.develop: Identity-shaped read demands autonomous execution without asking for permission or surfacing options. Role: Senior Data partner who owns the forecast delivery end-to-end. Fix the immediate gap (attach now) AND patch the workflow (add Excel to standard Slack output). Deliver both without asking which one he meant.
hypotheses.deliver: Immediate: find and post the Excel to the forecast Slack channel now. Permanent: update the forecast bot's Slack output logic to always attach the Excel file alongside the text summary. Report back with two lines: (1) 'Excel posted to #channel', (2) 'Bot updated — Excel will auto-attach on all future forecasts.' No deliberation, no options list.

hypotheses.label: Recall-shaped
hypotheses.deconstruct: PR321 documents the Forecast Lambda (Picanova, AWS) which generates reports with Summary columns and deploys iteratively. The EU+RoW Forecast Report (MEMORY.md) was built for a sales meeting. The forecast Slack bot appears to be a separate layer that calls or triggers the Lambda. The timeout likely occurred in the Lambda or in the bot's wait loop. The Excel is the Lambda's output artifact. Recall also surfaces PR330 (WhatsApp bridge 500 errors) — pattern: output pipelines have health-check failure modes that require explicit recovery steps.
hypotheses.diagnose: The Forecast Lambda generates an Excel (and possibly HTML). The Slack bot posts status but didn't attach the file — either because the Lambda timed out before producing it, or because the bot was not coded to attach the Excel. If the Lambda timed out, the file may not exist yet. If the bot simply skipped attaching, the file exists and just needs to be posted.
hypotheses.develop: Technical + chain-of-thought. First determine whether the Excel was actually generated (check Lambda output location / S3 / local path). If it exists → post it. If it doesn't → re-trigger the Lambda, wait for completion, then post. The Slack MCP tools can handle the post. The Lambda is on AWS (boto3 or direct invoke).
hypotheses.deliver: Step 1: Check whether the forecast Excel was generated — query S3 or the Lambda's output path for the most recent file. Step 2a (file exists): use Slack MCP to attach it to the forecast channel. Step 2b (file missing): re-invoke the Forecast Lambda via AWS MCP, wait for completion, then attach output. Step 3: Confirm in Slack with a brief message. Report outcome in two sentences.

hypotheses.label: Contrarian
hypotheses.deconstruct: What if 'attach the excel' is not about the current timed-out run at all, but about the forecast channel's standard output format? The Slack message shows the bot posting text-only status updates. Justs may have been expecting Excel attachments on every forecast output and is now surfacing this gap because the timeout made the missing file visible. The real ask is: change the forecast Slack integration so Excel is always included — the timeout is incidental.
hypotheses.diagnose: If this read is correct, manually posting the Excel now is the wrong action — it's a one-time patch that leaves the root format gap open. The diagnosis shifts from 'where is the file?' to 'why does the bot not attach the file by default?' This requires inspecting the forecast bot's Slack output code rather than just finding an artifact.
hypotheses.develop: Contrarian read demands adversarial check against the obvious interpretation. If the bot code is accessible, inspect whether Excel attachment was ever implemented. If it was never implemented (not a regression but a gap), then the fix is additive code, not a one-off file post. Role: architect reviewing delivery format requirements.
hypotheses.deliver: Check the forecast bot's Slack posting code — does it ever attach an Excel file? If not, the ask is a feature addition, not a file-find. Implement: after forecast generation, call Slack files.upload (or equivalent MCP tool) with the Excel path and post to the forecast channel. Deploy. Then post the current Excel manually as the immediate fix while the code change propagates. Report: 'Gap confirmed — bot never attached Excel. Code patched + current file posted.'

hypotheses.label: Minimal-action
hypotheses.deconstruct: Smallest action that satisfies: find the most recently generated forecast Excel file and post it to the Slack channel visible in the screenshot. No code changes, no Lambda re-trigger unless necessary, no architecture discussion.
hypotheses.diagnose: Single blocker: need the Excel file path and the Slack channel ID. Both are resolvable without user input — file via filesystem/Drive search, channel via the bot context or Slack search.
hypotheses.develop: Occam's razor: two tool calls solve this. (1) Locate file. (2) Post to Slack. Everything else is scope creep.
hypotheses.deliver: Run: find the most recent forecast .xlsx on EC2 or Drive. Then use slack_send_message (with file attachment) to post it to the forecast channel. Done in two steps. No code changes, no follow-up questions, no status explanation.
